methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $100M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Transportation. Range: $270M - $1.8B.

data.narrative:
Arc Boats, a Series B transportation company founded by Mitch Lee and Ryan Cook, has raised $100M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a universe of 8 private comparables in the transportation sector, with no direct public peers. Three same-sector, same/adjacent-stage companies—Einride (Series C, $1.8B), Harbinger (Series C, $500M), and REGENT Craft (Series A, $270M)—anchor the peer median analysis.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $100M total raised as a base proxy and applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical for Series B advancement, yielding $190M. Peer-median analysis across the three comparables produces a median valuation of $500M, with a range of $270M to $1.8B reflecting stage and performance dispersion within transportation. The peer set spans Series A through Series C, introducing stage heterogeneity as a source of variance.

Triangulating these two methods produces a valuation range of $162M (low) to $575M (high), with a median of $345M. The gap between methods reflects uncertainty around appropriate stage multiples and peer comparability. Key sensitivities include stage-relative positioning within the peer cohort and the accuracy of $100M raised as a valuation proxy. The peer range itself ($270M–$1.8B) underscores wide dispersion in the sector.
